data_flow: entity-details-renderer.js invokes window.entityDetailsAPI.getEntityDetails(entityType, entityId, { includeLinkedItems: true }) when opening the modal
EntityDetailsAPI.getEntityDetails() calls fetchWithRetry() -> fetchEntityFromAPI() to retrieve entity core data
If the backend payload lacks linked_items, getEntityDetails() triggers getLinkedItems(entityType, entityId)
getLinkedItems() applies UnifiedCacheManager (memory layer) before issuing GET /api/linked-items/<entity_type>/<entity_id>
Response is normalized by flattening child_entities and parent_entities into allLinkedItems array
Normalization keeps only id, type, title, description, status, created_at, updated_at fields — dropping side, investment_type, quantity, price, condition, etc.
Normalized array is cached (memory TTL 5m) and returned to renderer
entity-details-renderer.js -> _renderLinkedItemsTableBody() iterates through allLinkedItems and delegates per-row rendering to _renderLinkedItemRow()
_renderLinkedItemRow() expects type-specific properties (e.g., alert.condition, alert.target_value, execution.side, execution.quantity, execution.price)
Because the aggregator trimmed the payload, type-specific cells fall back to empty strings, so alerts/executions lose critical data while cash_flows/notes/tickers still render

cache_layers.backend: Flask blueprint decorated with cache_for and EntityDetailsService internal caching
